title: Find the inverse of a 2x2 matrix
description: Find the inverse of a 2x2 matrix.
topic_path: Find the inverse of a 2x2 matrix

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
7 & 9
\\\\
1 & 1
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
7 & 9
\\\\
1 & 1
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{7\cdot 1-9\cdot 1}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
1&-9
\\\\
-1&7
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
1&-9
\\\\
-1&7
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac92
\\\\
\dfrac12 & -\dfrac72
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac92
\\\\
\dfrac12 & -\dfrac72
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & -8
\\\\
-2 & 7
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & -8
\\\\
-2 & 7
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{2\cdot 7-(-8)(-2)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
7 & 8
\\\\
2 & 2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{2}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
7 & 8
\\\\
2 & 2
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac72 & -4
\\\\
-1 & -1
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac72 & -4
\\\\
-1 & -1
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & -8
\\\\
-5 & -5
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & -8
\\\\
-5 & -5
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-4(-5)-(-8)(-5)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & 8
\\\\
5 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{20}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & 8
\\\\
5 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
0.25 & -0.4
\\\\
-0.25 & 0.2
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
0.25 & -0.4
\\\\
-0.25 & 0.2
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & 2
\\\\
-6 & 6
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & 2
\\\\
-6 & 6
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-4\cdot 6-2(-6)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
6 & -2
\\\\
6 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{12}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
6 & -2
\\\\
6 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac16
\\\\
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac16
\\\\
-\dfrac12 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-3 & 6
\\\\
2 & -5
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-3 & 6
\\\\
2 & -5
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-3(-5)-6\cdot 2}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & -6
\\\\
-2 & -3
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & -6
\\\\
-2 & -3
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac53 & -2
\\\\
-\dfrac23 & -1
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac53 & -2
\\\\
-\dfrac23 & -1
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & -10
\\\\
6 & 9
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & -10
\\\\
6 & 9
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-10\cdot 9-(-10)\cdot 6}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
9 & 10
\\\\
-6 & -10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{30}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
9 & 10
\\\\
-6 & -10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-0.3 & -\dfrac13
\\\\
0.2 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-0.3 & -\dfrac13
\\\\
0.2 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-9 & -6
\\\\
0 & 2
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-9 & -6
\\\\
0 & 2
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-9\cdot 2-(-6)\cdot 0}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & 6
\\\\
0 & -9
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{18}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & 6
\\\\
0 & -9
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac19 & -\dfrac13
\\\\
0 & \dfrac12
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac19 & -\dfrac13
\\\\
0 & \dfrac12
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
1 & 4
\\\\
4 & 9
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
1 & 4
\\\\
4 & 9
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{1\cdot 9-4\cdot 4}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
9 & -4
\\\\
-4 & 1
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{7}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
9 & -4
\\\\
-4 & 1
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac97 & \dfrac47
\\\\
\dfrac47 & -\dfrac17
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac97 & \dfrac47
\\\\
\dfrac47 & -\dfrac17
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & 0
\\\\
-10 & -6
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-4 & 0
\\\\
-10 & -6
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(-4)(-6)-(-10)\cdot 0}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-6 & 0
\\\\
10 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{24}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-6 & 0
\\\\
10 & -4
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac14 & 0
\\\\
\dfrac{5}{12} & -\dfrac16
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac14 & 0
\\\\
\dfrac{5}{12} & -\dfrac16
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-7 & 0
\\\\
-6 & 3
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-7 & 0
\\\\
-6 & 3
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-7\cdot 3-0(-6)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
3 & 0
\\\\
6 & -7
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{21}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
3 & 0
\\\\
6 & -7
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac17 & 0
\\\\
-\dfrac27 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac17 & 0
\\\\
-\dfrac27 & \dfrac13
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & 7
\\\\
-1 & 2
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-5 & 7
\\\\
-1 & 2
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{-5\cdot 2-7(-1)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & -7
\\\\
1 & -5
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=-\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
2 & -7
\\\\
1 & -5
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac23 & \dfrac73
\\\\
-\dfrac13 & \dfrac53
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac23 & \dfrac73
\\\\
-\dfrac13 & \dfrac53
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
10 & 0
\\\\
-8 & 1
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
10 & 0
\\\\
-8 & 1
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{10\cdot 1-0(-8)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
1 & 0
\\\\
8 & 10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{10}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
1 & 0
\\\\
8 & 10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
0.1 & 0
\\\\
0.8 & 1
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
0.1 & 0
\\\\
0.8 & 1
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
5 & -3
\\\\
6 & -3
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
5 & -3
\\\\
6 & -3
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{5(-3)-6(-3)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-3 & 3
\\\\
-6 & 5
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-3 & 3
\\\\
-6 & 5
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-1 & 1
\\\\
-2 & \dfrac53
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-1 & 1
\\\\
-2 & \dfrac53
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & 1
\\\\
2 & -1
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & 1
\\\\
2 & -1
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{(-10)(-1)-1\cdot 2}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-1 & -1
\\\\
-2 & -10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{8}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-1 & -1
\\\\
-2 & -10
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac18 & -\dfrac18
\\\\
-\dfrac14 & -\dfrac54
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac18 & -\dfrac18
\\\\
-\dfrac14 & -\dfrac54
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

assessment_items.question_content:
Consider this matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
3 & 6
\\\\
-10 & -10
\end{array}\right]$

**Find the inverse of the matrix.**  
*Give exact values. Non-integers can be given as decimals or as simplified fractions.*

 
[[☃ matrix 1]]
hints:
Given the general matrix $\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]$, we can use the following formula to find its inverse:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
a&b
\\\\
c&d
\end{array} \right]^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{ad-bc}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
d&-b
\\\\
-c&a
\end{array} \right]$

Notice that $ad-bc$ is the determinant of the matrix.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\left[\begin{array}{c}
3 & 6
\\\\
-10 & -10
\end{array} \right]^{-1}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{3(-10)-6(-10)}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & -6
\\\\
10 & 3
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{30}\cdot
\left[\begin{array}{c}
-10 & -6
\\\\
10 & 3
\end{array} \right]
\\\\
&=\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac13 & -\dfrac15
\\\\
\dfrac13 & \dfrac{1}{10}
\end{array} \right]
\end{align}$

This is the inverse matrix:

$\left[\begin{array}{c}
-\dfrac13 & -\dfrac15
\\\\
\dfrac13 & \dfrac{1}{10}
\end{array} \right]$

[[☃ explanation 1]]
